# 5. KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 Part A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Q1. `[KTU University Exam - Dec 2023]`
**List any three advantages of Google Compute Engine over traditional on-premise virtualization.**

**Model Answer (3 points, ~50 words each):**

1. **Per-second billing** — Pay only for what you use, even by the second, after a 1-minute minimum. On-premise servers are billed yearly for capacity that may sit idle.
2. **Automatic scaling & global reach** — Provision VMs in any of 40+ regions instantly. On-premise requires hardware procurement and data-center expansion.
3. **No upfront CapEx** — GCE converts CapEx to OpEx. No depreciation, no power/cooling costs.

---

### Q2. `[KTU University Exam - May 2024]`
**What is a Preemptible VM in GCE? When should it be used?**

**Model Answer (3 points):**

A **Preemptible VM** is a short-lived compute instance that can be terminated by GCP at any time, with a maximum lifetime of 24 hours. It is offered at up to **60–80% lower price** than regular VMs.

**Use cases:**
- **Batch processing** (e.g., Hadoop/Spark jobs that can be checkpointed and restarted).
- **Fault-tolerant distributed workloads** (e.g., CI/CD build workers, scientific simulations).
- **Stateless, easily-restartable containers** (Kubernetes node pools).

> [!WARNING]
> Preemptible VMs should **NOT** be used for databases, single-replica stateful services, or any workload that cannot tolerate sudden termination.

---

## 5.2 Part B — Full 14-Mark Questions (Internal Choice)

### Question A `[KTU University Exam - May 2024]`

**(a)** Explain the architecture of Google Compute Engine with a neat block diagram. Discuss the resource hierarchy in GCP. **(7 marks)**

**(b)** With a suitable example, explain how **Sustained Use Discounts (SUDs)** and **Committed Use Discounts (CUDs)** are calculated. Show the difference between additive and multiplicative discount application. **(7 marks)**

### Model Answer for Q(a) — 7 Marks

**Step 1 — High-level architecture (2 marks)**
GCE's architecture has three logical planes:
- **Control Plane** (Google's Borg-based scheduler) — makes placement decisions.
- **Data Plane** (the host servers running KVM) — actually executes the VMs.
- **Storage Plane** (Persistent Disks, Local SSDs, Cloud Storage).

**Step 2 — Block diagram (2 marks)**
Refer to the architecture diagram in Section 4.3 above. (Examiner's note: drawing the layered diagram with Hypervisor → VM → Persistent Disk earns full marks.)

**Step 3 — Resource hierarchy (3 marks)**
Explain Organization → Folder → Project → Resource. Emphasize:
- IAM is inherited top-down.
- VPC networks are **per-project** by default but can be **Shared VPC** across projects.
- Billing is aggregated at the project level but can be linked to a billing account at the organization level.

### Model Answer for Q(b) — 7 Marks

**Step 1 — Define SUD (2 marks)**
SUD is **automatic**. When a VM is used for more than 25% of a month, GCP applies a tiered discount (20/40/60%).

**Step 2 — Define CUD (2 marks)**
CUD is a **commitment contract** for 1 or 3 years. Discounts: ~25% for 1-year, ~52% for 3-year on N2.

**Step 3 — Worked example showing multiplicative vs additive (3 marks)**

Consider an `n2-standard-4` VM running 80% of the month, with a 1-year CUD of 25%.

- Raw cost: $200
- If **multiplicative** (correct): $200 \cdot (1-0.60) \cdot (1-0.25) = 200 \cdot 0.4 \cdot 0.75 = \$60$
- If **additive** (wrong): $200 \cdot (1-0.60-0.25) = 200 \cdot 0.15 = \$30$ ← This is WRONG. GCP applies multiplicative.

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Pitfall Warning**: Many students incorrectly add SUD and CUD discounts. This is the single most common mistake. Always remember: **discounts multiply, they do not add**.

---

### Question B (Alternative Choice) `[KTU University Exam - Dec 2023]`

**(a)** What is **Live Migration** in GCE? Explain the iterative pre-copy algorithm with a diagram. **(7 marks)**

**(b)** Compare **GCE, AWS EC2, and Azure VMs** on at least six parameters. Which one would you recommend for a high-performance, GPU-intensive ML training workload, and why? **(7 marks)**

### Model Answer for Q(a) — 7 Marks

**Definition (2 marks)**: Live migration is the process of moving a running VM from one physical host to another **without stopping the workload** — typically used during host maintenance, firmware updates, or hardware failures.

**Algorithm steps (3 marks)**:
1. Reservation
2. Iterative pre-copy (memory iterations)
3. Stop-and-copy (pause)
4. Network redirection (ARP trick)
5. Resume on destination

**Diagram (2 marks)**: Refer to Section 4.2 (Live Migration Flow Mermaid diagram).

### Model Answer for Q(b) — 7 Marks

**Comparison table (3 marks)**: Use the matrix in Section 3.2 (cover at least 6 rows).

**Recommendation for GPU-intensive ML (4 marks)**:
- **GCE with A2 machine types** is recommended because:
  - Up to **16× NVIDIA A100 GPUs** per VM.
  - **Per-second billing** saves cost during model warmup/cooldown.
  - Tight integration with **Vertex AI** and **TPU v4 Pods** for hybrid CPU+GPU+TPU training.
  - **100 Gbps egress** for moving large datasets to Cloud Storage.

> [!WARNING]
> **Pitfall**: Students often recommend AWS (p4d.24xlarge) or Azure (NDv5) but **fail to justify the discount model**. Always mention *why* the pricing/billing model of your chosen provider suits the workload.

---

## 5.3 Topic Recap — Important Things to Remember

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Rapid Revision Checklist for GCE (Module 4):**

- ✅ GCE is Google's **IaaS** offering, providing **virtual machines** on demand.
- ✅ Built on **KVM hypervisor** running on Linux hosts in Google's private data centers.
- ✅ **Four-level hierarchy**: Organization → Folder → Project → Resource.
- ✅ **Machine type families**: E2 (cheap), N2 (balanced), M2 (memory), C2 (compute), A2 (GPU).
- ✅ **Custom machine types** are a unique GCE feature (per-vCPU, 256 MB RAM granularity).
- ✅ **Per-second billing** with **1-minute minimum** is a competitive advantage.
- ✅ **SUD = automatic, tiered (20/40/60%)**, applies to the *entire* on-demand price once the threshold is crossed.
- ✅ **CUD = contractual, 1- or 3-year**, up to 57% discount.
- ✅ **Discounts multiply**, not add.
- ✅ **Preemptible / Spot VMs**: up to 80% cheaper, max 24-hour lifetime, can be killed anytime — use for fault-tolerant batch jobs.
- ✅ **Live migration** uses iterative pre-copy + final stop-and-copy; downtime typically < 1 second.
- ✅ **Persistent Disks** are zonal by default; **Regional PDs** provide synchronous replication across two zones.
- ✅ **Local SSDs** are physically attached, ephemeral, extremely fast (good for swap, scratch).
- ✅ **Confidential VMs** use AMD SEV (N2D) for memory encryption — security edge.
- ✅ **Service Accounts** give VMs identity to call Google APIs (Storage, BigQuery, etc.).
- ✅ **gcloud CLI** is the standard way to create/list/manage VMs from the terminal.
- ✅ **Regions** contain **zones** — design for HA by placing replicas in *different zones, different regions*.
